mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in <55% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, reducing aggregate corporate revenues and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no statistically significant difference in forward returns compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals near-term earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in <50% of rising INDPRO periods during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 200, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict return direction with an accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which translates into improved corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces business investment and inventory restocking, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
